fact: August 6 is exactly 218 days into the year, which means there are only 147 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak butterfly season - monarchs are getting ready for their epic 3,000-mile journey to Mexico!
The sun rises around 6 AM and sets around 8 PM in most of North America, giving you about 14 hours of daylight to play outside.
August 6 falls smack in the middle of Leo season - people born today are supposedly brave like lions and love being the center of attention!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest right now in many places - some make sounds as loud as a motorcycle engine!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, August 6 is deep winter, with temperatures that can drop below freezing.
This date sits exactly 45 days after the summer solstice in the north, meaning daylight is shrinking by about 2 minutes each day.
Perseid meteor showers are ramping up around this time - you might spot shooting stars if you stay up late and look northeast!
Many fruit trees are dropping their ripest peaches, plums, and apricots right now, making sidewalks sticky and attracting swarms of wasps.
Ocean water temperatures hit their warmest point of the year in many coastal areas, perfect for swimming but also prime time for jellyfish blooms.
Bats are having feeding frenzies on August nights because insect populations peak during these hot, humid weeks.
The moon phase on August 6 changes every year, but when it's a new moon, the night sky becomes perfect for stargazing since there's no moonlight to wash out the stars.
